title: ferray-core: indexing always returns IxDyn — loses compile-time rank
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

When you call index_axis() on an Array<T, Ix2>, the result is ArrayView<T, IxDyn> instead of ArrayView<T, Ix1>. Similarly, slice_axis on Ix3 returns IxDyn instead of Ix3. This is because the ndarray RemoveAxis machinery doesn't map cleanly to ferray's Dimension trait. NumPy doesn't have this problem because rank is always dynamic. But ferray's selling point IS compile-time rank safety, and losing it at every indexing operation undermines the value proposition. Investigate whether associated types or const generics can preserve rank through index_axis (Ix2 -> Ix1), slice_axis (IxN -> IxN), insert_axis (IxN -> Ix(N+1)).
